```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, if x.width < PAD then PAD else x.width)
}

lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); 
    c.kind == x.kind &&
    c.value == x.value &&
    c.width >= PAD &&
    (x.width >= PAD ==> c.width == x.width) &&
    Wellformed(c) &&
    (Canonicalize(c) == c)
{ }
```

The implementation is straightforward: `Canonicalize` preserves the kind and value, and sets the width to the maximum of the current width and `PAD` (which is 4).

The ensures clauses specify that:
1. The kind is preserved
2. The numeric value is preserved
3. The canonical width is at least 4
4. If already at 4+ digits, the width is unchanged
5. The result is wellformed
6. Canonicalizing an already-canonical id is idempotent (a no-op)
